law.text:
Chapter 1. Small companies

Art. 1:24. Small companies

par. 1. Small companies are companies with legal personality that on the balance sheet date of the last closed financial year do not exceed more than one of the following criteria:
(1) annual average number of employees: 50;
(2) annual net turnover as referred to in article 1:26/1, exclusive of value added tax: 11,250,000 euros;
(3) balance sheet total: 6,000,000 euros.

par. 2. When more than one of the criteria referred to in paragraph 1 are exceeded or are no longer exceeded, this only has consequences when this occurs during two consecutive financial years. The consequences shall in that case take effect from the financial year following the financial year during which more than one of the criteria were exceeded or were no longer exceeded for the second time.

par. 3. For companies that are starting their business, for the purpose of applying the criteria mentioned in paragraph 1, these figures shall be estimated in good faith at the beginning of the financial year. If this estimate indicates that more than one of the criteria will be exceeded during the first financial year, this must be taken into account immediately for that first financial year.

par. 4. If the financial year exceptionally has a duration of less or more than twelve months, whereby this duration cannot be longer than twenty-four months minus one calendar day, then the amount of the net turnover exclusive of value added tax referred to in paragraph 1 shall be multiplied by a fraction of which the denominator is twelve and the numerator is the number of months of the financial year concerned, whereby each commenced month is counted as a full month.

par. 5. The average number of employees employed, referred to in paragraph 1, is the average of the number of employees expressed in full-time equivalents registered in the DIMONA database in accordance with the royal decree of 5 November 2002 introducing an immediate declaration of employment, with application of article 38 of the law of 26 July 1996 on the modernisation of social security and safeguarding the viability of the statutory pension schemes, at the end of each month of the financial year, or if the employment does not fall within the scope of application of this royal decree, the average number of employees employed expressed in full-time equivalents of the employees registered in the general personnel register or an equivalent document at the end of each month of the financial year under consideration.

The number of employees expressed in full-time equivalents is equal to the volume of work expressed in full-time employed equivalents, to be calculated for part-time employees on the basis of the contractual number of hours to be performed, related to the normal working time of a comparable full-time employee.

When the income arising from the ordinary business of a company consists for more than half of income that does not correspond to the description of the net turnover item, then for the purpose of paragraph 1, net turnover shall be understood to mean: the total of operating and financial income with the exclusion of non-recurring income.

The balance sheet total referred to in paragraph 1 is the total book value of the assets as it appears from the balance sheet format established by royal decree adopted in execution of article 3:1, paragraph 1.

par. 6. If the company is affiliated with one or more other companies as referred to in article 1:20, the criteria regarding net turnover and balance sheet total referred to in paragraph 1 shall be calculated on a consolidated basis. With regard to the criterion of number of employees, the number of employees, calculated according to the provisions of paragraph 5, that each of the affiliated companies concerned employs on an annual average, shall be added together.

If, when calculating the threshold amounts referred to in paragraph 1, the eliminations referred to in the royal decree adopted in execution of article 3:30, paragraph 1, and any resulting omission are not carried out, then these threshold amounts concerning the balance sheet total and the net turnover shall be increased by twenty percent.

par. 7. Paragraph 6 does not apply to companies other than parent companies as referred to in article 1:15, (1), except where such companies have been established with the sole purpose of evading the reporting of certain information.

For the purpose of this paragraph and paragraph 6, companies forming a consortium as referred to in article 1:19 shall be equated with a parent company.

Art. 1:25. Micro-companies

par. 1. Micro-companies shall be understood to mean: small companies with legal personality that are not a subsidiary or parent company and that on the balance sheet date of the last closed financial year do not exceed more than one of the following criteria:
(1) annual average number of employees: 10;
(2) annual net turnover referred to in article 1:26/1, exclusive of value added tax: 900,000 euros;
(3) balance sheet total: 450,000 euros.

par. 2. Article 1:24, paragraphs 2 to 5 and paragraph 8, shall apply.

kb_used.fo:
vocabulary V {
  type Company
  type FinancialYear
  has_legal_personality: Company -> Bool
  employee_criterion_exceeded: Company * FinancialYear -> Bool
  turnover_criterion_exceeded: Company * FinancialYear -> Bool
  balance_sheet_criterion_exceeded: Company * FinancialYear -> Bool
  more_than_one_small_company_criterion_exceeded: Company * FinancialYear -> Bool
  is_small_company: Company * FinancialYear -> Bool
  is_last_closed_financial_year: Company * FinancialYear -> Bool
  annual_average_employees: Company * FinancialYear -> Int
  annual_net_turnover_excl_vat: Company * FinancialYear -> Real
  balance_sheet_total: Company * FinancialYear -> Real
}

theory T:V {
  ! c in Company, y in FinancialYear: ((is_last_closed_financial_year(c,y)) & (annual_average_employees(c,y) > 50)) => ((employee_criterion_exceeded(c,y))).
  ! c in Company, y in FinancialYear: ((is_last_closed_financial_year(c,y)) & (annual_net_turnover_excl_vat(c,y) > 11250000.0)) => ((turnover_criterion_exceeded(c,y))).
  ! c in Company, y in FinancialYear: ((is_last_closed_financial_year(c,y)) & (balance_sheet_total(c,y) > 6000000.0)) => ((balance_sheet_criterion_exceeded(c,y))).
  ! c in Company, y in FinancialYear: ((employee_criterion_exceeded(c,y)) & (turnover_criterion_exceeded(c,y))) => ((more_than_one_small_company_criterion_exceeded(c,y))).
  ! c in Company, y in FinancialYear: ((employee_criterion_exceeded(c,y)) & (balance_sheet_criterion_exceeded(c,y))) => ((more_than_one_small_company_criterion_exceeded(c,y))).
  ! c in Company, y in FinancialYear: ((turnover_criterion_exceeded(c,y)) & (balance_sheet_criterion_exceeded(c,y))) => ((more_than_one_small_company_criterion_exceeded(c,y))).
  ! c in Company, y in FinancialYear: ((has_legal_personality(c)) & (is_last_closed_financial_year(c,y)) & (~more_than_one_small_company_criterion_exceeded(c,y))) => ((is_small_company(c,y))).
  ! c in Company, y in FinancialYear: ((is_last_closed_financial_year(c,y)) & (more_than_one_small_company_criterion_exceeded(c,y))) => ((~is_small_company(c,y))).
}

case.text: NV Tango has legal personality. On the balance sheet date of the last closed financial year: 60 full-time equivalents, annual net turnover 12,000,000 euros (excluding VAT), balance sheet total 5,000,000 euros.

questions.text: Is NV Tango a small company pursuant to Article 1:24, paragraph 1?

expected.reason: Under Art. 1:24 par. 1 a small company may exceed at most one threshold; Tango exceeds employees (>50) and turnover (>11250000).

employee_criterion_exceeded.name: employee_criterion_exceeded
employee_criterion_exceeded.description: The employee criterion is exceeded for the company on the balance sheet date of the given financial year.

turnover_criterion_exceeded.name: turnover_criterion_exceeded
turnover_criterion_exceeded.description: The turnover criterion is exceeded for the company on the balance sheet date of the given financial year.

balance_sheet_criterion_exceeded.name: balance_sheet_criterion_exceeded
balance_sheet_criterion_exceeded.description: The balance sheet criterion is exceeded for the company on the balance sheet date of the given financial year.

more_than_one_small_company_criterion_ex.name: more_than_one_small_company_criterion_exceeded
more_than_one_small_company_criterion_ex.description: More than one small-company criterion is exceeded for the company in the given financial year.

is_small_company.name: is_small_company
is_small_company.description: The company qualifies as a small company based on legal personality and the small-company criteria in the given financial year.

is_last_closed_financial_year.name: is_last_closed_financial_year
is_last_closed_financial_year.description: The given financial year is the last closed financial year for the company.

annual_average_employees.name: annual_average_employees
annual_average_employees.description: The annual average number of employees of the company in the given financial year.

annual_net_turnover_excl_vat.name: annual_net_turnover_excl_vat
annual_net_turnover_excl_vat.description: The annual net turnover exclusive of value added tax of the company in the given financial year, in euros.

balance_sheet_total.name: balance_sheet_total
balance_sheet_total.description: The balance sheet total of the company in the given financial year, in euros.

routing.question: Is NV Tango a small company pursuant to Article 1:24, paragraph 1?

deduction.label: contradicted
deduction.constraint: ? x0 in Company, x1 in FinancialYear: __sel0(x0) & __sel1(x1) & is_small_company(x0,x1)

symbolic_intent_routing.question: Is NV Tango a small company pursuant to Article 1:24, paragraph 1?

pipeline.natural_language: No. Nv_tango, Last_closed_fy does not satisfy: The company qualifies as a small company based on legal personality and the small-company criteria in the given financial year. (If you expected Yes, ensure case facts use the exact predicate names from the KB schema.)
